adjudication_raw:
Review: Two-stage music recommendation — neural retriever evaluation.

Verdict: critique_wins

The Recall@100 improvement (0.43 vs. 0.31) conflates three advantages: supervision richness (impression-level negatives vs. positive-only), feature richness (audio embeddings + skip behavior vs. play-only), and neural architecture. No intermediate baseline tests matrix factorization with the same supervision source or features. The paper cannot demonstrate neural retrieval quality when the comparators differ in inputs and training signals. The internal reviewers' concern is valid and unanswered.
